This program is co-sponsored with ACSA.

events.description: Join us this PA Day at the library for a screening of the family movie - Zootopia 2. To crack the case, Judy and Nick must go undercover to unexpected new parts of town, where their growing partnership is tested like never before. Rated PG. 1hr 48 mins. English Subtitles for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (SDH) when available.

Drop in!*

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime
events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: Wonder Workshop: Introduction to Design Thinking Building Challenge
events.description: Have you ever wondered what it would be like to be an inventor?  Have you ever thought about building something yourself?  What is something you would like to build?  Is there a problem you would like to fix?  Use your imagination and brainstorm creative solutions to develop a machine that will fix a problem or make a task easier for you!  Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book. For kids ages 4 to 8 with their parents/caregivers.

events.title: Family Movie: GOAT
events.description: Join us this Summer at the library for a screening of GOAT movie. The story follows Will, a small goat with big dreams who gets a once-in-a-lifetime shot to join the pros and play roarball--a high-intensity, co-ed, full-contact sport dominated by the fastest, fiercest animals in the world. Will's new teammates aren't thrilled about having a little goat on their roster, but Will is determined to revolutionize the sport and prove once and for all that 'smalls can ball!' Rated PG. 1hr 40mins. Engli

events.description: Mr. Eric's Musical Adventures is a music lesson packaged in silly fun and nonsense! Through highly engaging and catchy original songs and interactive audience participation, Mr. Eric teaches rhythm, rhyming, song writing, singing, movement, and pitch, with a big boost of vocabulary to boot! While he’s spent years as a professional orchestra conductor, Mr. Eric has found a special kind of joy in sharing music and stories with young audiences! For children ages 4-8. Drop in.

events.title: Wonder Workshop - Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book.  For kids ages 4 to 8. Space is limited. Registration begins June 1st. Information Collection Notice: The perso

events.title: Learn Not to Burn: Fire Safety for Children
events.description: Join the Toronto Fire Service in this special visit to teach our young community members about fire safety and prevention, and what to do when there is an emergency. Drop-in program for children and families. No registration required, space is limited.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.description: Looking for something FUN to do on Sundays! Come to our hands-on and collaborative learning program series; Lego Challenge, Robotics, Snap Circuits, and Board Games. For ages 6-12!

events.description: Come join us and explore electricity and circuitry. Learn about circuits and gain hands on experience with SNAP Circuits! Feel the spark when you build your own circuit! For children ages 6-12. Drop in.

events.description: Step into The Curious Chaos Club- where creativity gets a little messy (in the best way!) Kids ages 6- 12 can build, create and let the chaos begin!

events.description: Join us as we celebrate all fathers and make a craft to take home to them. All Ages. Drop–in only. No registration required.

events.description: Explore a different STEM-themed activity every Sunday the library is open. Topics include robotics, circuitry, building and more! For children aged 6-12 and their caregivers. Drop in.

events.description: Explore the world of circuitry and robots. Test out Snap Circuits, Dash robots, or Spheros. Ages 5 and up.

events.description: Join us for our weekly STEAM Lab (Ages 6-12) , where kids can explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through fun, hands-on activities. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Sunday Fun-Day

No prior experience is needed—just bring your curiosity! Drop in. No registration required, but space is limited.

We'll explore plotting and characters so you can improve your writing skills and craft amazing stories of your own. Kids 8-12.

events.title: Story & Film: Jonathan Cleaned Up
events.description: Jonathan is trying not to make a mess, but someone has put a subway station in his apartment! To figure out what's going on, Jonathan goes to City Hall and discovers something about the City Hall computer that just might help him fix his problem. Join us for a storytime with the Robert Munsch story Jonathan Cleaned Up…Then He Heard a Sound and a screening of the animated version from the National Film Board, Blackberry Subway Jam . Drop-in.

Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! For ages 0-12.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Summer Lego Challenge
events.description: Looking for an afternoon challenge? Come out to our Summer Lego Challenge for hands on learning about design and engineering, while building towers, bridges, boats and more! For ages 6-12.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Summer BINGO
events.description: For children ages 6-12. Cool off and play a few rounds of Summer Bingo for fun and the chance to win cool books, stickers and more. No registration – but ticket is required. Space is limited. Tickets will be handed out at the 2 nd floor desk 15 minutes before the program starts.

events.title: Fictional Character Bingo and TD Summer Reading Club Draw
events.description: Let's finish off this summer with a fun round of Bingo! This time, we'll be doing popular fictional characters in lieu of numbers. Have you read lots of stories? Maybe you can guess who they are too! Prizes will be given out for Bingo winners! Also, if you've completed the TD Summer Reading Club challenge of finishing 12 books, we'll be doing a draw for special prizes at the end of the program. Drop-in!

events.title: Yoga Storytime with Appleseed Yoga
events.description: ​Join us for an interactive, movement-based program where we read stories and make them come alive with our bodies! Appleseed Yoga for Kids will deliver inspiring stories that get the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or materials required. Drop-in with limited space. All

events.title: Wonder Workshop - Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book.  For kids ages 4 to 8. Space is limited. Registration begins June 1st. Information Collection Notice: The perso

events.description: Get ready to mix, mold, and fizz. In this hands-on program, kids will become junior scientists as they explore the chemistry behind bath bombs. events.title: Builder's Club
events.description: Join us on Saturday mornings and build to your heart’s content!  LEGO, DUPLO, and KEVA planks will be provided for you to explore your creativity. All children welcome with their caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Sunday Crafternoon
events.description: Join us for Sunday crafts. Different activity each week. Sundays 12-4:30 pm. Drop-in.

events.title: SUNDAY FUN-DAY
events.description: Looking for something FUN to do on Sundays! Come to our hands-on and collaborative learning program series; Lego Challenge, Robotics, Snap Circuits, and Board Games. For ages 6-12!

events.title: Sunday Fun-day
events.description: Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and games. Drop-In.
events.location: Oakwood Village Library and Arts Centre

events.title: Sunday Family Crafts
events.description: Come as a family and enjoy making crafts together. This program is for families or caregivers with children. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: After School Club
events.description: Looking for something to do after school? Come out to our After School Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids and have fun! For ages [6-12]. Snacks are offered during the After School Club for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: Story Planet Comic Lab
events.description: The Artists in the Library series comes to Evelyn Gregory Library this Fall!  Led by professional illustrators Hilary Leung and Joseph Bonsu, children will explore the art of comics and build a portfolio of work culminating in a polished long-form comic. The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Game on! Board Game Playtime
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled day of board games and puzzles! Play your favourites or try something new! Brings your friends and family or meet new people at the library. events.title: Solar System Stories: A Merril Collection Exhibit
events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. It opens on Tuesday, May 19 following the holida

events.title: Branch Art Exhibit: When The Cavas Speaks-Artistic Journeys by Sarah Chi
events.description: Throughout the month of June, works by artist Sarah Chi are on display at Northern District Library branch in an exhibit. Sarah is her school’s Art Club Head, a TPL Teen Council and Young Voices Council member. She has created art since age six and began expressing personal ideas at fourteen. Working in oil, acrylics, ink, printmaking, and mixed media, she has won Scholastic Art & Writing Awards (Gold and Silver Key) and had work published in Young Voices magazine. She co-founded

events.title: Soccer Scavenger Hunt
events.description: Fort York is full of soccer! Drop by the branch to check out our June Scavenger Hunt. Search the first floor for soccer balls and win a special 3D printed prize when you find them all!

events.title: PA Day Family Movie: Zootopia 2
events.description: Join us this PA Day at the library for a screening of the family movie - Zootopia 2. Detectives Judy Hopps and Nick Wilde find themselves on the twisting trail of a mysterious reptile who arrives in Zootopia and turns the mammal metropolis upside down. To crack the case, Judy and Nick must go undercover to unexpected new parts of town, where their growing partnership is tested like never before. Rated PG. 1hr 48 mins. English Subtitles for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing (SDH) when available.

events.title: PA Day: Exploratory Instrument Zoo
events.description: Join us this PA Day to learn about the cello, violin and percussion instrument! Participants will be encouraged to explore, play and hear the following musical instruments! The following session will be lead by Sistema Toronto Teaching Artists to demonstrate and instruct the interaction with the following instruments. Afterwards, the following instruments can be borrowed from the branch’s Musical Instrument Lending Library. For Children of All Ages. Registration is Required.  Please registe

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Hillcrest Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Registration is required. The tour will start and end at the Hillcrest Library and will be guided by the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff. There m

events.title: Pawsitively Pets
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program that offers positive animal interactions for children and adults of all ages. Join us and enjoy interacting with a variety of animals, while learning about the responsibilities of pet ownership! Children attending this event must be accompanied by an adult. When registering, please indicate how many children are attending. This program can accommodate up to 25 children.  Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this f

events.title: Sewing Basics
events.description: Want to learn to sew or would like a refresher on how to use a sewing machine? Our intro to sewing course will cover the basics of threading the machine, basic stitches and more. We will be creating a small pouch. All materials will be provided. Children under 13 must be accompanied by an adult. Registration is required for this class and begins May 22nd, 2026 at 12:30 pm. Note: Your spot will not be guaranteed after 5 minutes of the start of the program. Information Collection Notice: The perso

events.title: Youth Hub: The Sticker Room Project
events.description: A hands-on creative program where teens can design, decorate, and personalize art projects using stickers while socializing and expressing their creativity in a fun and welcoming environment. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Henna Art Workshop *Teens Only*
events.description: Discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna. Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Colouring
events.description: Come to the Youth Hub with your friends and practice mindfulness with some peaceful colouring. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Cricut Tumbler Cups
events.description: Stay cool and hydrated this summer with your own personalized tumbler cup! No registration required but supplies are limited. Ages 13 - 17. events.description: Exams are stressful! Come to the Parliament Street Youth Hub for a quiet spot to study. During this time, there will be no video games available. Study supplies will be provided. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Rainbow Loom Bracelets
events.description: Join us for a fun-filled afternoon where you will learn the simple technique of weaving small rubber bands to create unique and vibrant bracelets. Whether you want to decorate your own wrists with color or design the perfect gift for a friend, we will show you how.

events.title: Virtual Reality Demo (13 +)
events.description: This Drop-In demonstration is for patrons over the age of 13 years old. Leap into the world of virtual reality at the library! Join this drop-in session to try out the Meta Quest virtual reality headset and immerse yourself in stunning 360-degree environments, engage in captivating adventures, and interact with the virtual world.

events.title: POV: You Found a Good Book
events.description: Not your average book club. Think less assigned reading, more good vibes, real talk, and stories that actually hit. No pressure, no quizzes—just snacks, chill convos, and books you might actually want to finish. Come hang out, meet people your age, and help pick what we read next. You might even find a book you don’t hate.  Drop in. Bring a friend. Or just bring yourself—we saved you a spot. The Hate U Give

events.title: All Ages Karaoke and Board Game Night

You are also welcome to bring your own and invite others to play with you. Children must be accompanied by an adult.

events.description: A-Frame is a web framework for creating virtual reality (VR) experiences with HyperText Markup Language (HTML), allowing developers to create 3D and VR content using familiar HTML markup that works across desktop or laptop computers, mobile devices, and VR headsets.  This basic introduction involves typing code to create simple objects and environments in the online code editor CodePen. events.description: Python is an open source programming language that is used for creating web applications, web content, mathematics and system scripting. events.description: TV host and celebrity chef Sang Kim will teach the basics of making Korean-style kimbap, exactly like you see them on your favourite K-dramas. A brief history of kimbap, its differences from the Japanese sushi roll, and eating etiquette will also be shared. Prizes are also awarded for best kimbap creations. events.title: Let's Learn About Computer Hardware
events.description: This hands-on workshop series introduces the process of building a gaming desktop, from selecting components to installing an operating system. events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Finding a Job with ACCES Employment
events.description: Workplace Readiness & Soft Skill This session focuses on essential workplace skills such as communication, teamwork, time management, and professionalism. It is designed to help youth understand workplace expectations and succeed in their first roles. For teen sessions, bringing a resume is optional. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available.&nbs

This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: LivingWorks SafeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks SafeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks SafeTALK certificat

events.title: Disassemble a Desktop Computer
events.description: Have you ever wondered what's going on inside a computer? Ever wanted to take apart a computer to see? This workshop is your chance! Free Geek Toronto is a non-profit organization dedicated to getting unwanted computers and electronics back into working condition. During this workshop, we'll go over what's inside a computer, how to properly open a desktop, and safety guidelines for working with electronics.

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.
